name: Exchange_Mech

infobox.Systems: Force Swap(Science)Effect ()Force a nearby unit to swap places with any other unit.Upgrade IHeal Ally ()Swapping an ally will heal it completely.Upgrade IIHurt Enemy ()Swapping an enemy will deal 1 damage to it.

sections.Team: The Mech is available as part of theBombermechssquad.
sections.Strategy:
The Exchange Mech can use Force Swap to trade spots with any two targets on the battlefield. This can allow two Vek to miss their targets, or potentially hit each other. Handling two threats in a single action makes it very strong, with the added utility of being able to swap with any other second threat that is hard-to-reach making it invaluable.
Since Force Swap has strong utility to start, it doesn't demand extra cores immediately on any of its upgrades. That said, the Exchange Mech starts with some of the worst stats in the game; at the start of the timeline, it only has 3 movement, it's grounded, and it's also quite frail at 2 HP. Thus, spending at least one core on +1 Move for the Exchange Mech should be considered a priority, in order to reach valuable Swap targets more easily.Heal Allycan be considered more pressing to get if the squad is using weapons such asSelf-DestructorIce Generator(this Repair will revive downed Mechs and remove status effects). Otherwise, the ability to swap anywhere on the battlefield makes its other upgrades less critical than with other Mechs.
Much like with theTeleporterand similar Science class weapons, check the Attack Order before using Force Swap, in order to turn Vek attacks against each other and maximize damage, potentially having them neutralize each other. In the Bombermechs squad, the player can utilize Bomblings from theBombling Mechas swap targets, offering a lot of flexibility in exactly where an attacking Vek can be placed. This can be done before the Walking Bomb moves, as well to putting it in a better position for the Walking Bomb to move afterwards. Easy instant kills can also be garnered by moving one of your mechs into a hazardous tile such as water, and swapping it with a ground-based Vek. A Mech without strong positional requirements (for e.g. the Bombling Mech) is an ideal target for this. Similarly, Vek can be swapped with to block their spawn points; this deals damage to them, blocks the spawn, and generally moves them away from buildings.
The Exchange Mech is also a very flexible Mech for anyCustom Squad, thanks to its very strong starting utility. A particularly notable Mech that synergizes well is theGravity Mech, as the increased damage fromVek Hormonesis very effective at forcing swapped targets to take each other out. But in general, the utility of swapping two targets in the battlefield makes it suitable for any combination.
As with most Science mechs,Kazaaakplethis a good Pilot that offers valuable damage and push for turns where the swap is not required. Otherwise, look for pilots offering move and/or health, as the Exchange Mech needs to be able to reach it's target to offer the greatest possible utility to the squad.
